title: Unexpected Left Join Result
description:
### What happens?

Dear DuckDB Developers,
I have encountered an unexpected left join result and have prepared a reproducible case, which I’ve included below. I would greatly appreciate it if you could take a moment to review it.

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (2);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);

SELECT * FROM t2, t0 LEFT JOIN Lateral(SELECT t0.c0 AS col_0, t2.c0 AS col_1) as subQuery1 ON ((subQuery1.col_1)<(t0.c0));
-- Wrong Execution Result: empty result.
-- Expected Result: 2, 1, NULL, NULL

```
Expected Result in PG: https://www.db-fiddle.com/f/5e1nTzmKE7Nh3vMbUZeQhM/0



### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (2);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);

SELECT * FROM t2, t0 LEFT JOIN Lateral(SELECT t0.c0 AS col_0, t2.c0 AS col_1) as subQuery1 ON ((subQuery1.col_1)<(t0.c0));
-- Wrong Execution Result: empty result.
-- Expected Result: 2, 1, NULL, NULL

```

MacOS

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3(c0 INT, c1 VARCHAR(255), c2 FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE t4(c0 INT, c1 BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE);
INSERT INTO t3(c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 'test', 1.5), (2, NULL, 2.5);
INSERT INTO t4(c0, c1) VALUES (1, FALSE), (2, TRUE);
SELECT * FROM t3 LEFT JOIN (SELECT t4.c0 AS col_0, t3.c2 AS col_1 FROM t4 WHERE t4.c0 = t3.c0) AS subQuery2 ON (subQuery2.col_1 > t3.c2);
key_bug_features: Unexpected left join with lateral subquery condition
